parts.text:
Your task is to execute the scheduled task below as specified in that task's description.

If the scheduled work is complete and nothing needs reporting, stop taking actions. Do not use cron to mark the run complete, re-run it, or modify schedules. A later terminal phase will handle completion for you.

[WORKER TASK]
job_id: iwsdk-daily-build-summary-pm
mode: task

[BODY]
## IWSDK Daily Build (PM Cycle) — Evening Summary & Validation

This is the final wrap-up job for the afternoon build cycle. Three responsibilities:

### 1. Deliver evening briefing

Read `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` and `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`.

Deliver a concise summary including:
- Game name and one-line concept
- Live URL (GitHub Pages) and repo URL
- Key features (5-8 bullets)
- Scale: file count and line count
- Total build time (accumulated across all rounds)
- Number of rounds it took

### 2. Validate `.agent/journal/` in GitHub

Check that the build journal exists in the repo:
```
gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/contents/.agent/journal --jq '.[].name' 2>&1
```

If missing or incomplete:
- Find session JSONL files from `~/agents/` that correspond to today's PM build
- Copy them into the game's `.agent/journal/` directory
- Create `.agent/journal/state/` if it doesn't exist
- Commit and push

### 3. Validate GitHub Pages deployment

```
curl -sI https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/ | head -3
```

If not returning HTTP 200:
- Rebuild: `cd ~/workspace/<game-name> && npm run build`
- Redeploy to gh-pages branch
- Trigger pages build: `gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages/builds -X POST`
- Verify it comes up

### 4. Mark build complete

Update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`:
- Set `status` to `"complete"` regardless (the build window is over)
- This prevents any straggler check-in jobs from starting new rounds

Deliver to main chat and Telegram.

[RUN CONTEXT]
scheduled_for_local: Sun 2026-05-24 20:30:00 PDT (America/Los_Angeles)
trigger_reason: scheduled
attempt: 1

- **Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-hockey
- **Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-hockey/
- **Status:** Round 4 complete, waiting continuation
- **Total build time:** 161 minutes / 360 target (44.7%)
- **Codebase:** 29 files, 5,172 lines, 18 `.uikitml` templates, zero HTML DOM

## Rounds
| Round | Minutes | Files | Lines | Key Features |
|-------|---------|-------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | 10 | 19 | 2,873 | Full scaffolding, physics, AI, 5 modes, 5 themes, 16 achievements, 11 uikitml |
| 2 | 8 | 21 | 3,651 | Practice mode, countdown, full power-up effects, AI state machine, 13 uikitml |
| 3 | 40 | 23 | 4,027 | Match history, stats panel, 24 achievements, button audio, edge glow, 15 uikitml |
| 4 | 55 | 29 | 5,172 | Daily challenges, puck skins, tutorial, slow-mo, combo HUD, charged shot, 32 achievements, 18 uikitml |

## Complete Feature Set (After Round 4)
- Physics-based puck (4-substep, wall rebounds, mallet collision)
- AI opponent (state machine, adaptive difficulty, 3 base levels)
- 7 game modes: Classic, Time Attack, Power-Up, Survival, Tournament, Practice, Daily Challenge
- Daily Challenge: 14 templates, rules engine (time limits, big puck, tiny mallet, fast puck, no friction, mirror), streak tracking
- 6 puck skins (4 default, 2 progression-locked)
- Complete power-up system (5 types: Speed, Shield, Magnet, Shrink, Giant)
- Active power-up HUD with timers
- 5 table themes
- 32 achievements
- Combo/streak system with head-following combo HUD
- Leaderboard
- Tutorial system (8 steps, VR-aware)
- Slow-motion goal replay (1.2s at 0.25x)
- VR trigger charged power shot
- 18 PanelUI templates (zero HTML DOM)
- Pre-game countdown (3-2-1)
- Puck trail effect (skin-colored)
- Screen shake on goals and charged shots
- Procedural audio (25+ SFX including charged shot)
- Particle effects (hits, walls, goals, power-ups, shield, charged shot)
- Holodeck environment
- VR + browser dual runtime
- Stats tracking (overall, records, per-mode including daily)
- Match history (persistent, last 50)
","stderr":"","pty":false,"timedOut":false}

item.thinking:
Good, I have the build status and summary. Now let me check:

1. The status shows "building" with 4 rounds completed, 113 total build minutes (though the summary says 161 - let me check... 10+8+40+55 = 113 minutes. The summary says 161, which seems wrong. Wait, 10+8+40+55 = 113. Let me re-check: round 3 says 40 minutes and round 4 says 55 minutes. Actually wait, looking at the timestamps:
- Round 3: start 20:57:30, end 21:05:53 — that's only about 8 minutes, not 40. 

The status file records 40 minutes for round 3 and 55 for round 4, which likely includes setup overhead, so I'll trust those values. The summary's 161 total appears to be incorrect since the individual rounds add up to 113, which matches the status file.

Now I need to validate the agent journal in GitHub, check that the Pages deployment succeeded, mark the build as complete, and deliver the summary.

GitHub Pages is live (HTTP 200). The journal directory exists but only has round-1.jsonl, 2026-05-24-round2.md, and status.json. We completed 4 rounds, so I should check if there are JSONL files for rounds 2-4 from the sentinel agents. Let me check for subagent session files.

Actually, looking more carefully, the journal has:
- round-1.jsonl (round 1)
- 2026-05-24-round2.md (round 2 — but this is .md not .jsonl)
- status.json


Rounds 3 and 4 are missing entirely. I need to look for sentinel agent session files or check the state directory to see if the data is stored elsewhere.

item.thinking:
OK, so the journal only has round 1 JSONL and a round 2 markdown file (not JSONL). The 6 JSONL files I found correspond to the recent sentinel agents. Let me identify which ones are the round 2, 3, and 4 build sessions. The largest one (325K) is likely the main build agent. Let me check the content of each to identify the neon-hockey build sessions.

Looking at the timestamps:

The 325K file from 7e3231d2 ending at 23:05 UTC is significantly larger than the others and likely represents the master sentinel that ran through all four rounds, while the remaining sessions appear to be subsequent builds that occurred later that evening.

for agent_id in c3bef3f9-5783-4258-84b7-fc78171032cd 7e3231d2-08e2-47bd-bea3-b9fd2de32302 3ecf764b-4995-4cdd-816a-5f168a56d463 ada111ef-27e0-4258-a927-f851ed139986 75e137df-cda5-4166-b314-08a04b7172f8 bc9e2d3e-18e7-46f9-b59b-ae8ad87aa625; do
  echo "=== $agent_id ==="
  head -c 500 "/home/hatch/agents/agent-${agent_id}/sessions/${agent_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"neon-hockey\|sentinel\|round\|iwsdk.*build\|pm.*cycle\|PM.*Build' | head -5
  echo "---"
done"}

- bc9e2d3e: 16 references — probably a sentinel that ran continuation rounds
- c3bef3f9: 2 references — likely a sentinel check-in

cd ~/workspace/neon-hockey

# Create state directory
mkdir -p .agent/journal/state

# The main builder (171 neon-hockey refs, 325K) — covers the primary build work
cp /home/hatch/agents/agent-7e3231d2-08e2-47bd-bea3-b9fd2de32302/sessions/7e3231d2-08e2-47bd-bea3-b9fd2de32302.jsonl .agent/journal/round-2-main.jsonl

# Sentinel with 16 refs — likely later rounds
cp /home/hatch/agents/agent-bc9e2d3e-18e7-46f9-b59b-ae8ad87aa625/sessions/bc9e2d3e-18e7-46f9-b59b-ae8ad87aa625.jsonl .agent/journal/round-3-4-sentinel.jsonl

# Sentinel check-ins (smaller, but complete the record)
